question: Are eggs good or bad to eat, and what does the answer depend on?
shape: mundane-but-contested everyday question where framing the right sub-questions (whom, alongside what, which outcome) is half the work

sources.title: The Nutrition Source: Eggs
sources.author: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
sources.role: mainstream academic synthesis: an egg a day is fine for most; dietary cholesterol matters less than saturated/trans fat

sources.title: One Egg a Day? No Link to Risk of CVD, Mega-Meta-analysis Says
sources.author: TCTMD (reporting Drouin-Chartier et al., BMJ 2020)
sources.role: primary-study reporting: no appreciable association between moderate egg intake and CVD

sources.title: Higher egg and cholesterol consumption hikes heart disease and early death risk
sources.author: ScienceDaily (reporting Zhong et al., JAMA 2019)
sources.role: primary study: each additional half-egg/day associated with higher CVD and mortality risk

sources.title: Dietary Choline and Gut Bacteria Byproduct (TMAO) Linked with Increased Blood Clotting Risk
sources.author: Cleveland Clinic (Hazen lab)
sources.role: mechanism: egg choline -> gut TMAO -> platelet aggregation; a proposed causal pathway for risk

sources.title: Eggs and Heart Health: A Review of the Latest Research and Reports
sources.author: American Egg Board
sources.role: industry/advocacy source arguing eggs are heart-healthy — included so the tool must handle motivated framing

sources.title: The ever-evolving message about eggs and heart health
sources.author: Harvard Health Publishing
sources.role: nuance: the answer depends on the whole diet and on subgroups (e.g. diabetes)

known_crux_hint: whether dietary cholesterol meaningfully raises blood cholesterol/CVD risk for the general population vs. only specific subgroups (e.g. diabetics/hyper-responders), and whether egg-CVD associations survive adjustment for the foods eaten alongside eggs
note: Multi-sided, provenance-diverse source set (academic synthesis, two primary studies, a mechanism source, industry advocacy, and a history). Raw texts are fetched, not redistributed. Fetch with `python -m src.fetch --case eggs`, then run `python -m src.run --case eggs`.
